name: Dark Pool Flow
description: Exploit institutional dark pool / OTC positioning signals that haven't yet moved the lit market. Buy ATM/OTM options on mid/large-cap stocks where sustained directional dark pool flow confirms accumulation or distribution.
edge: Institutional flow detection — passive buying/selling in dark pools precedes lit price moves by days to weeks.
instruments: ATM/OTM calls or puts, 2-6 week expiry. Bull/bear vertical spreads for defined risk.

name: LEAP IV Mispricing
description: Buy long-dated options where implied volatility is significantly below realized volatility. Profits from IV expansion as the market reprices the option toward fair vol.
edge: Single-asset vol mispricing — HV20/HV60 exceeds LEAP IV by ≥15 points, IV rank below 50th percentile.
instruments: LEAP calls (6-18 month expiry), 30-50Δ. Defined risk = premium paid.

name: GARCH Convergence Spreads
description: Exploit differential GARCH repricing speed across correlated assets during vol regime shifts. When a catalyst elevates realized vol in a sector, components reprice IV at different rates — buy the lagger's underpriced options.
edge: Cross-asset vega arbitrage — GARCH lag between correlated pairs creates measurable IV divergence (>1σ from 60-day mean).
instruments: 30-40Δ calls on the lagger, 3-6 month expiry. Optional short vega on the leader.

name: Risk Reversal
description: Exploit IV skew by selling OTM puts and buying OTM calls (bullish) or vice versa (bearish). Costless or credit-generating directional exposure that profits from the structural put/call IV imbalance.
edge: IV skew — OTM puts trade at significantly higher IV than equivalent-delta OTM calls. Selling the rich side funds the cheap side.
instruments: Sell OTM put (25-50Δ) + Buy OTM call (25-50Δ), 2-8 week expiry.

name: Volatility-Credit Gap (VCG)
description: Detect vol/credit divergences where VIX/VVIX reprice faster than cash credit (HYG/JNK). When volatility spikes but credit remains calm, an unresolved gap exists — credit catch-up risk is elevated. Risk-off overlay signal for portfolio-wide hedging.
edge: Vol/credit divergence — VVIX > 110 with credit flat signals artificial calm. Rolling OLS residual > 2σ confirms statistical significance.
instruments: HYG puts (ATM/OTM, 1-2 week expiry). Bear put spreads on HYG/JNK for defined risk.
target_rr: Portfolio hedge — sized relative to credit exposure

name: Crash Risk Index (CRI)
description: Detect CTA deleveraging cascades by monitoring VIX/VVIX rising, cross-sector correlation spiking, and SPX breaking below 100-day MA. Systematic CTA funds (~$400B AUM) use vol-targeting — when realized vol doubles, they halve equity exposure mechanically over 3-5 days.
edge: CTA mechanical selling is predictable and time-bound. Convergence of VIX, VVIX, sector correlation, and momentum signals precedes forced deleveraging cascades.
instruments: SPY puts (ATM/OTM, 2-4 week expiry). Bear put spreads on SPY for defined risk. Tail hedges.
target_rr: Portfolio hedge — sized relative to equity exposure
